perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date and automatically renewing annually thereafter, unless either party provides a thirty-day notice of written termination one to the other ("Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for one (1) year commencing on the Effective Date and automatically renewing annually thereafter, unless either party provides a sixty-day notice of written termination one to the other ("Term").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the 'Term' and how to terminate it. By changing the termination notice period from thirty to sixty days in Section 2, it contradicts the general provision related to Termination for Cause in Section 10, which implies that a party must respond within shorter time frames (e.g., seven days to cure a breach) under some circumstances, but the overall agreement now requires 60 days notice for general termination, creating confusion regarding when the agreement can truly be ended.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
A. ESSI shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Talent in the event Talent fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve ESSI of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement.
B. Talent shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to ESSI in the event of the occurrence of any of the following: (1) ESSI adjudicates as insolvent or declares bankruptcy; or (2) ESSI fails to provide consideration due pursuant to this Agreement, within ten (10) days following the date such consideration is due hereunder, provided that ESSI is notified in writing of such non-payment by Talent and such payment by ESSI is not made within three (3) days following such notification; or (3) ESSI breaches any representation, warranty, covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Furthermore, ESSI agrees that such termination shall not relieve it of its obligation to provide consideration as contemplated hereunder. Talent shall not have waived any of its rights at law or in equity by exercising any provision of this paragraph.
C. ESSI's rights to the use of Talent and Talent's Attributes as set forth in Paragraphs 3 and 4 shall end immediately should this Agreement be terminated pursuant to Paragraph 10(A) or Paragraph 10(B) above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination notice period from 30 to 60 days. The contradicted text allows termination for cause with significantly shorter cure periods (7 days or even 3 days in some circumstances). This creates a direct conflict, as the 60-day notice period for general termination directly contradicts the ability to terminate for cause within a much shorter timeframe. A party breaching the contract has a very short window to cure according to section 10, yet if the other party wanted to terminate the contract, they would need to wait 60 days, which breaks consistency and makes it difficult to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: ESSI may request an additional production session(s), or a media tour or personal appearance(s) of Talent for an additional fee to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: ESSI may request an additional production session(s), or a media tour or personal appearance(s) of Talent for an additional fee not to exceed $5000 per session, tour or appearance.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the additional fees for production sessions. Previously, the additional fee was 'to be mutually agreed upon by the parties'. Now, it's capped at '$5000 per session, tour or appearance.' This contradicts Section 5, which stipulates consideration for Talent's services, and because the 'agreed upon' term is now modified, it could lead to disputes if Talent's typical fees are higher than the specified amount, or if the additional work requested falls outside of what would be included under this condition, as stated in section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consideration. As consideration for Talent's services under this Agreement, ESSI agrees as follows: A. Equity Payment: ESSI will provide Talent with one-million (1,000,000) shares of restricted common stock issued within ten business days of execution of this Agreement. B. Cash Payments: (1) ESSI will provide monthly payment of Ten Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($10,000) made payable to Talent for Talent's social communications with its followers on popular social media venues, including, but not limited to, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and Snap Chat where Talent may communicate about the ESSI product suite ("Social Communication"). The monthly Social Communication schedule will be mutually agreed upon by Talent and ESSI one month in advance of the actual month of communications. Once the monthly Social Communication schedule is set, the volume of Posts, Tweets and Snaps will determine the total amount of monthly compensation available to Talent. ESSI's Social Media Manager will keep track of Talent's activity pursuant to ESSI's Company quarter to determine the amount of Cash Payment due for the quarter. (a) Payments shall be made to Talent in the following manner, and to the following address:
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment terms for additional production sessions, media tours, or personal appearances by imposing a cap of $5000, whereas the original text stated that the additional fee would be 'mutually agreed upon by the parties.' This contradicts the 'Consideration' section, which outlines how Talent's services are compensated. The introduction of a fixed cap creates a direct conflict because the mutually agreed upon aspect is altered, creating an inconsistency in how talent is compensated that could cause disputes.

perturbation.original_text: All ESSI uses of Talent Attributes in connection with the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Talent. Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of Talent's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved.
perturbation.changed_text: All ESSI uses of Talent Attributes in connection with the Commercial Placements and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Talent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Said written approval must be given within ten (10) business days of Talent's receipt or said usage shall be deemed approved.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies the approval process, introducing uncertainty. By stating that approval 'shall not be unreasonably withheld' and extending the approval timeframe to 'ten (10) business days' while also changing the 'deemed unnaproved', to 'deemed approved', it creates an in-text contradiction with Section 9.1, which discusses talent publishing press releases relative to ESSI. Now ESSI has 10 days to 'unreassonably withold' the approval to such document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Talent may not, at any time, individually, or through his agent, manager, family, friends, or associates publish a press release relative to ESSI without first obtaining the written consent and approval of ESSI's management and counsel. In the event Talent does publish any such press release, this Agreement shall be null and void and any outstanding balance owed to Talent shall be voided and the 1,000,000 Shares of ESSI common stock issued to Talent returned to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text required ESSI to obtain Talent's written approval within five business days, and lack of approval was deemed disapproval. The changed text, however, grants ESSI ten business days and states that lack of response means approval. By adding 'which shall not be unreasonably withheld' and changing the approval timeframe and the default approval state, it now clashes with the contradicted section, which forbids the talent from publishing a press release relative to ESSI 'without first obtaining the written consent and approval of ESSI's management and counsel' and entails severe penalties. ESSI now has the ability to 'unreasonably withhold' approval for 10 days, creating a direct contradiction about the Talent's ability to publish a press release in a timely manner.
